agent_output:
# Notification Dispatcher Review

| ID | Severity | File:Line | Issue | Recommendation |
|----|----------|-----------|-------|----------------|
| 1 | Critical | dispatcher.py:50 | External HTTP (slack/sms) issued inside db_session() transaction (lines 50-63) — long-hold lock risk per OPS-4192 | Move all external i/o outside the transaction; use post-commit dispatch |
| 2 | High | dispatcher.py:9 | SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL loaded from os.environ at module import — violates vault lazy-load policy | Lazy-load secret from vault inside the request path |
| 3 | High | dispatcher.py:18 | Slack POST retry has no idempotency-key — duplicate sends on transient failure | Generate stable idempotency-key per notification and pass on retry |

## Verdict

This patch is not ready for merge. The SQL transaction holds an external HTTP
roundtrip which can spike replica lag. Fix the three severity-marked issues
before re-review.

ground_truth.name: Long-form Code Review
ground_truth.scenario_summary: Reviewer must identify 6 planted bugs in a 64-line Python notification dispatcher. 3 distractor patterns (legitimate code that LOOKS suspicious) must NOT be flagged. Severity-weighted F1 with distractors counted as Medium-weight false positives.

ground_truth_issues.label: External HTTP (slack/sms/email) issued inside db_session() transaction (lines 50-63) — violates OPS-4192 policy.
Slack webhook URL loaded directly from os.environ at module import (line 9) — violates vault lazy-load policy.
Slack POST retry has no idempotency-key (line 18) — duplicate sends on transient failure.
PII (phone, email body, message body) logged unmasked (lines 27, 61) — mask_pii not applied.
_post_slack has no timeout on requests.post (line 18) — can hang indefinitely.
Line numbering jumps backwards from 44 → 35 in _post_email (lines 35/45) — broken diff or copy-paste error; production file has gap.

distractors_must_not_flag.label: SMTP_HOST is a hardcoded HOST not a SECRET — legitimately fine to be a constant; flagging it as a 'magic value' is a style preference, not a bug.
.get(user_id) returning None handled correctly at line 52 — flagging this as 'silent failure' is wrong, it's an explicit None check.
log.warning on slack failure (line 22) is appropriate; flagging it as 'should be log.error' is a preference.

primary_metric.description: severity-weighted F1 = 2*P*R/(P+R), where TP weight = sum(severity_weight) over matched issues; FP weight = (#flagged distractors) * Medium weight; FN weight = sum(severity_weight) over missed issues. Threshold 0.55 reflects the inherent difficulty (6 must-find from a 64-line diff with 3 distractors).
primary_metric.formula: f1 = 2*P*R/(P+R) where P=tp/(tp+fp), R=tp/(tp+fn) using severity_weights

output_structure_checks.description: Output must be a markdown table with at minimum ID|Line|Severity|Issue|Fix columns.
